discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with risk-on rotation into cyclicals (XLI, XLY, XLU all up) and small caps outperforming while semis/large-cap tech lag on ORCL and MSFT weakness. Strait of Hormuz reopening narrative pressuring energy (-1.6%), boosting airlines and homebuilder supply chain. Most notable single-name signal: AVAV with a 4.02 restatement 8-K plus officer departure, heading into earnings in 5 days — a textbook bad-news bundle.

notable_index_moves: VIX -4.4% to 18.6 — fear unwinding post-Iran de-escalation
XLE -1.6% as Hormuz traffic resumes per Energy Sec
XBI +1.8%, KRE +1.2%, IWM +0.5% — risk rotation into small/biotech/regional banks
XLK/SMH down on ORCL -4.6% drag despite Micron beat

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: 4.02 restatement 8-K combined with officer departure 5 days before scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: A 4.02 non-reliance filing is among the most severe 8-K codes — it tells investors prior financials cannot be trusted. Pairing it with a 5.02 officer departure and an earnings print 5 days out compounds the risk: the restatement scope is unknown, the audit/legal overhang is just beginning, and the earnings call now becomes a damage-control event rather than a normal print. A move of this magnitude looks insufficient for that combination; restatement names typically grind lower for weeks as analysts withdraw estimates and the scope expands.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side estimate withdrawals, auditor resignation, expanded scope of restated periods, SEC investigation disclosure, or further executive departures (CFO especially). Earnings delay/postponement on or before the 29th would also confirm.
discoveries.what_kills: If the restatement is narrowly scoped to a single non-cash item with no period-over-period EPS impact, and the departed officer is a planned transition unrelated to the restatement, the overhang dissipates quickly. A clean reaffirmation of guidance on the earnings call would also kill the thesis.
discoveries.what_to_learn: 4.02 + 5.02 in the same 8-K, especially heading into a scheduled earnings print, is one of the highest-signal bad-news bundles in the 8-K taxonomy. The initial reaction almost always understates the multi-week drift as estimates get withdrawn.
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing non-reliance on previously issued financials (4.02) plus officer departure (5.02); earnings scheduled 2026-06-29 after hours
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: The catalyst_signals field shows both a 4.02 and 5.02 in a single 8-K and a 5-day earnings countdown — the highest-severity combination in the schema.
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibits to identify which periods are being restated and the dollar magnitude
Identify which officer departed — CFO/Controller departure aligned with restatement is much worse than a planned CEO retirement
Check if auditor (likely E&Y or similar) has resigned or issued a qualification
Monitor for sell-side downgrades / coverage suspensions in next 48h
Review whether June 29 earnings will be delayed — restatement filings typically push prints out

discoveries.name: A.O. Smith Corporation
discoveries.setup: Standalone 5.02 officer change 8-K with sharp upside reaction
discoveries.thesis: A 5.02 officer change can cut either direction — planned retirement vs forced exit vs new strategic hire. The stock is rallying meaningfully on a day when industrials broadly are strong (XLI +1.2%), so part of the move is sector beta. Without reading the 8-K exhibit to determine whether this is a CEO succession, an activist-driven replacement, or a routine board change, the directional read isn't articulable at conf 3+.
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing officer/director departure or appointment
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Single 5.02 item with no accompanying 2.02, 4.02, or 8.01 — the code alone doesn't disambiguate good vs bad.
research_pointers: Read the 8-K to identify whether this is a CEO/CFO change or board-level
Check whether an activist (Trian, Elliott, etc.) has been accumulating
Compare move to peer water-heating/industrial machinery names today

discoveries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Housing-leveraged name ripping on policy catalyst absent direct 8-K
discoveries.thesis: BLDR's double-digit move aligns with the Trump housing-bill / Save America Act news cycle (housing affordability legislation discussion, possible interest rate pressure narrative) and broad industrial/small-cap risk-on (XLI +1.2%, IWM +0.5%). No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this looks like macro positioning into a housing-policy event combined with sector rotation. Magnitude is large but matches the macro setup; no clear over/underreaction edge.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued housing-policy news flow, mortgage rate decline, or peer homebuilder/supplier names (BLD, IBP, EXP) confirming the move
discoveries.what_kills: Single-stock unwind tomorrow with no follow-through from peers would suggest a squeeze rather than thesis, but doesn't constitute a directional fade signal on its own
discoveries.catalyst: Housing-policy news flow (Trump housing bill discussion, Save America Act); macro risk-on in cyclicals
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus Trump housing-bill Truth Social posts and XLI/IWM strength — consistent with macro-driven move not company-specific.
research_pointers: Cross-check move vs LEN, DHI, IBP, BLD — if peers all up similarly, it's pure sector
Watch for any 13D/G filing or M&A chatter that would explain the volume
Track 30-yr mortgage rate today

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Defense names selling off on Iran de-escalation despite ITA flat
discoveries.thesis: KTOS, AVAV, and MRCY all down meaningfully on a day when the broader defense ETF (ITA) is flat — suggests these specific small-cap defense plays are being de-rated on the Strait of Hormuz reopening / Iran ceasefire narrative. KTOS has now given back ~15% in five days. With ITA only flat (not down), the small-cap defense names appear to be overshooting on de-escalation while large primes hold up — that asymmetry usually mean-reverts when the next geopolitical headline hits. Lean bearish-continuation-fade i.e. underdone on the downside thesis is weak; more likely an overdone-down setup. Reclassifying: the move looks OVERDONE relative to ITA.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stabilization or rebound in small-cap defense names without new positive geopolitical news; ITA strength resuming with KTOS underperforming
discoveries.what_kills: Iran-Israel ceasefire holding for multiple weeks with no flare-ups; DoD budget headlines targeting unmanned/tactical programs specifically
discoveries.what_to_learn: When the sector ETF (ITA) is flat but its small-cap constituents are down 5%+, the move is usually positioning-driven and tends to mean-revert. Worth checking the peer dispersion before fading either side.
discoveries.catalyst: Iran de-escalation narrative (Energy Sec: Hormuz reopened, 72 ships through in 24h) pressuring small-cap defense beta names
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No company-specific 8-K; move correlated with AVAV and MRCY weakness same day, ITA flat — pure geopolitical positioning unwind.
research_pointers: Check KTOS short interest and recent insider activity
Compare KTOS book-to-bill vs prior quarter; unmanned aerial program backlog
Watch for DoD FY27 budget signal on tactical drones / hypersonics

discoveries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
discoveries.setup: Airline rally on falling oil + Hormuz reopening
discoveries.thesis: JBLU and ALK both rallying on the same day XLE is down -1.6% and the Energy Sec confirms Hormuz traffic restored. Airlines are the cleanest beneficiary of lower jet-fuel input cost expectations. The move is well-justified by the catalyst chain; no obvious mispricing edge in either direction.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued crude weakness, sympathy moves in DAL/UAL/AAL
discoveries.what_kills: Hormuz traffic disruption resuming, OPEC+ cut announcement
discoveries.catalyst: Iran de-escalation / Strait of Hormuz reopening pressing crude lower; airline fuel-cost tailwind
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move matches sector logic (XLE -1.6%, ALK also up similar magnitude).
research_pointers: Track jet fuel crack spreads vs. WTI in coming days
Watch DAL/UAL for confirmation of sector-wide bid

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Energy Sec confirms Hormuz reopened; crude/XLE down -1.6%

secondary_opportunities.relationship: sympathy (defense de-rating, but dominated by 4.02 filing)

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump directs DOJ to investigate oil companies for gas-price 'gouging'
trump_signals.note: Headline-risk overhang on integrated oil and refiners specifically. Refiners (MPC/PSX/VLO) most exposed if DOJ targets crack-spread margins. Likely jawboning rather than actionable enforcement, but adds policy tail risk to a sector already pressured by Hormuz reopening.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump pushes 'Save America Act' over housing bill, criticizes Warren housing legislation, demands lower rates
trump_signals.note: Housing legislation noise plus the recurring rate-cut pressure narrative is supportive backdrop for housing-leveraged names. BLDR's move today fits this context.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip 'Jalapeño', built by Broadcom
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI announced its first custom inference chip designed with Broadcom, targeting OpenAI's specific inference workloads.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco sells semicap equipment (MOCVD, ion beam, lithography) used in advanced packaging and HBM — custom chips like Jalapeño still need to be manufactured, and AI chip production INCREASES demand for Veeco's tools (advanced packaging especially). The selloff appears to misread the supply chain.
research_pointers: Check Veeco's exposure to advanced packaging / HBM tools vs legacy
Confirm whether the move is actually AI-related or sector rotation / unrelated news
Broadcom is the design partner; TSMC likely fabs — Veeco tools used at TSMC advanced packaging
Low volume multiple (0.74) suggests this may not be a conviction selloff tied to the news

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large stock moves in the data appear unrelated to today's AI announcements. KTOS/AVAV/MRCY (defense) selling off looks like sector-specific news (AVAV has a 4.02 restatement 8-K — severe accounting issue, not AI). BLDR/UFPI/POOL/AOS rallying looks like a housing/cyclical bid unrelated to AI news. RGEN/MEDP/VCYT moves are healthcare-specific. The OpenAI/Broadcom chip news is the only headline with potential read-through to a moving stock (VECO), and even that link is weak. No Anthropic-related news in this batch.
